briefdescription.para: Application device signal type @SINCE_1_2.62.
Application device signal type @SINCE_1_2.62.
Application device orientation changed signal type @SINCE_2_2.1.
Application lifecycle signal and system signal callback type @SINCE_1_0.0.
Application control signal callback type @SINCE_1_0.0.
This is the constructor for applications.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0 @PRIVLEVEL_PUBLIC @PRIVILEGE_DISPLAY
argc
A pointer to the number of arguments
argv
A pointer to the argument list
stylesheet
The path to user defined theme file (optional)
A handle to the Application
If the stylesheet is not specified, then the library's default stylesheet will not be overridden.

memberdef.argsstring: (int *argc, char **argv[], Dali::StringView stylesheet, bool useUiThread, WindowData &windowData)
memberdef.name: New

briefdescription.para: This is the constructor for applications with full window control.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_2.23 @PRIVLEVEL_PUBLIC @PRIVILEGE_DISPLAY
argc
A pointer to the number of arguments
argv
A pointer to the argument list
stylesheet
The path to user defined theme file
useUiThread
True if the application would create a UI thread
windowData
The window data for the application
A handle to the Application
If the stylesheet is not specified, then the library's default stylesheet will not be overridden. UI thread is an additional thread that DALi creates for UI events. The UI thread isn't blocked from the system events(AppControl, LanguageChanged, RegionChanged, LowMemory, LowBattery task signals).

briefdescription.para: Get path application resources are stored at.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.2
the full path of the resources

briefdescription.para: Constructs an empty handle. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Copy Constructor. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Assignment operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Move constructor.
Move assignment operator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_9.24
rhs
A reference to the moved handle
A reference to this handle

briefdescription.para: Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This starts the application.

detaileddescription.para: On platforms where context loss can occur, the application is responsible for tearing down and re-loading UI. The application should listen to Stage::ContextLostSignal and Stage::ContextRegainedSignal.
@SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: This lowers the application to bottom without actually quitting it. @SINCE_1_0.0.
This quits the application. Tizen applications should use Lower to improve re-start performance unless they need to Quit completely. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Ensures that the function passed in is called from the main loop when it is idle. @SINCE_1_0.0.

detaileddescription.para:
callback
The function to call
true if added successfully, false otherwise
Function must be called from main event thread only
A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(); This callback will be deleted once it is called.

simplesect.para: Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the main window used by the Application class.

detaileddescription.para:
The application writer can use the window to change indicator and orientation properties. @SINCE_1_0.0
A handle to the window

briefdescription.para: This is used to get region information from device.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.62
Region information

briefdescription.para: This is used to get language information from device.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_2.62
Language information

briefdescription.para: Gets the Object registry.

The user should connect to this signal to determine when they should initialize their application. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.

The user should connect to this signal to determine when they should terminate their application. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.

The user should connect to this signal if they need to perform any special activities when the application is about to be paused. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.

The user should connect to this signal if they need to perform any special activities when the application has resumed. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.

This signal is sent when the system requires the user to reinitialize itself. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.
This signal is emitted when another application sends a launch request to the application.

detaileddescription.para:
When the application is launched, this signal is emitted after the main loop of the application starts up. The passed parameter describes the launch request and contains the information about why the application is launched. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the language is changed on the device. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.

This signal is emitted when the region of the device is changed. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_0.0.

This signal is emitted when the battery level of the device is low. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_2.62.

This signal is emitted when the memory level of the device is low. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread. @SINCE_1_2.62.
This signal is emitted when the device orientation is changed Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the UI thread. Otherwise, it is emitted on the main thread.

detaileddescription.para:
This signal is only used in Application, it is different to Window's orientation signal. @SINCE_2_2.1
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: The user should connect to this signal to determine when they should initialize their application. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.

The user should connect to this signal to determine when they should terminate their application. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.
This signal is emitted when another application sends a launch request to the application.

detaileddescription.para:
When the application is launched, this signal is emitted after the main loop of the application starts up. The passed parameter describes the launch request and contains the information about why the application is launched. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.
The signal to connect to

briefdescription.para: This signal is emitted when the language is changed on the device. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.
This signal is emitted when the region of the device is changed. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.
This signal is emitted when the battery level of the device is low. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.
This signal is emitted when the memory level of the device is low. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all.
This signal is emitted when the device orientation is changed. Only when the user uses the UI thread, this signal is emitted on the main thread. Otherwise, it is not emitted at all. @SINCE_2_2.1.
An Application class object should be created by every application that wishes to use Dali.

detaileddescription.para: It provides a means for initializing the resources required by the Dali::Core.

The Application class emits several signals which the user can connect to. The user should not create any Dali objects in the main function and instead should connect to the Init signal of the Application and create the Dali objects in the connected callback.
Applications should follow the example below:
If required, you can also connect class member functions to a signal:

sect4.title: UI thread
sect4.para: There is the UI thread feature. UI thread is an additional thread that an Application object creates. The thread is for UI events.

When the UI thread feature is enabled, you can use the task signals(TaskInit, TaskTerminate, TaskAppControl, TaskLanguageChanged, TaskLowBattery, and TaskLowMemory). The task signals are emitted on the main thread, and the normal signals(Init, Terminate, Pause, Resume, Reset, AppControl, LanguageChanged, Region, LowBattery, and LowMemory) are emitted on the UI thread.

If you want to handle windows or actors in cases like when the memory level of the device is low, you have to use the normal signals, not the task signals. Callbacks of all signals in DALi except the task signals are emitted on the UI thread. (e.g., Timer callbacks are emitted on the UI thread.)

To enable the UI Thread, you can use this method. you have to set True to the useUiThread. Dali::Application::New(int *argc, char **argv[], Dali::StringView stylesheet, bool useUiThread, WindowData& windowData)
This class accepts command line arguments as well. The following options are supported:
When the above options are found, they are stripped from argv, and argc is updated appropriately. @SINCE_1_0.0
